metadata.title:
MUCH NORTH
SIDE PAVING

p r OLE CTS CONTEMPLATED
THAT WILL EQUAL THREE
TIMES SIZE OF BROWNIES
ADDITION JOB.

pages.raw_text:
In the matter of street paving
during 1910, the north side prom
ises to keep pace with the rest of
the city. There are sufficient paw
ing propositions under contempla-
tion at this time, that If carried out
will equal three times the area of
the Brownies addition Job, esti-
mated to cost over 100000. Some
of these projects have been order
ed by the city council, petitions are
out for others, and still others are
in the formative period that pre-
cedeS the issuance of the paving
petition


The paving of Monroe street in
1908 and Division street in 1909 has
given an impetus to this character
of improvement on the north side,
and it will not be many years until
the chief streets and avenues in
the central section of the north
side are improved with paving.
Broadway has been ordered paved.
but work was delayed over the
strife on the 10-year maintenance
bonds. The contract is now ready
to be awarded.


North Howard street from the
river to Spofford avenue has been
ordered paved with brick. Property
owners on Washington street are
